meta.name: Pseudobulk differential expression (DESeq2)
meta.description: Replicate-aware differential expression between two conditions, one test per cell type: sum raw counts per (sample × cell type) into bulk-like profiles, run DESeq2, then draw an MA + volcano plot for one cell type.
meta.provenance: PyDESeq2 (Love et al., Genome Biol 2014) with pseudobulk aggregation (Squair et al., Nat Commun 2021); the replicate-aware alternative to per-cell rank_genes_groups for condition comparisons.

readme: Compares two conditions per cell type the statistically valid way — cells are aggregated to one pseudobulk profile per biological sample, and the samples are the replicates. Needs three obs columns already present: `sample_key` (replicate id), `condition_key` (the two-level contrast), and `celltype_key` (e.g. from 'Assign cell-type labels (CellTypist)'), plus at least 2 samples per condition per cell type (others are skipped). Requires RAW integer counts: leave `layer` blank to use `.X` on a freshly imported store, or point it at a raw-counts layer — do not run after normalizing `.X`. Results land in `uns[key_added]`; the plot shows the MA + volcano for one cell type (blank `cell_type` = the first available).

params.name: condition_key
params.tooltip: per-cell condition forming the two-level contrast

params.name: celltype_key
params.tooltip: per-cell type/cluster; one DESeq2 fit per level

params.name: layer
params.tooltip: raw-counts layer to aggregate (blank = .X); must be raw integer counts

params.name: cell_type
params.tooltip: which cell type's MA/volcano to plot (blank = the first available)

params.name: key_added
params.tooltip: uns key to store the differential-expression results under
